_meta.description: Per-repo seed context for the portfolio chat knowledge base. Generated by crawling package.json / config across the repos backing the PROJECTS array. ingest.ts embeds only entries where seed=true.
_meta.generatedFrom: github.com/tim-rayner repos mapped to the portfolio PROJECTS array
_meta.seedingRule: Public/own repos default to seed:true. Private repos containing client, employer, or third-party material default to seed:false — flip to true ONLY if you are certain the content is yours to publish on a public-facing bot.

entries.seedReason: Private commercial product under a Ynomia/PCE arrangement. Confirm what is yours to expose before enabling.
entries.title: Structura
entries.purpose: Digital-twin construction assistant for QA inspection and handover documentation. Renders BIM/IFC models in the browser and layers progress tracking, evidence capture, and an LLM chat assistant over them.

stack.framework: Next.js 16 (App Router) + React 19 with the React Compiler
stack.data: Drizzle ORM over postgres-js against Supabase Postgres; Supabase SSR auth
stack.bim3d: That Open Engine (@thatopen/components, components-front, fragments) + web-ifc + web-ifc-three on Three.js for IFC parsing and 3D rendering
stack.ai: OpenAI SDK for the chat/orchestration layer; react-markdown to render responses
stack.ui: MUI v7 + Emotion, Framer Motion, Lenis smooth scroll, dnd-kit drag-and-drop

entries.methodologies: Type-safe end-to-end API contract via tRPC + Zod; deterministic geometry code with the LLM used only for orchestration; offline-first caching of query state; WASM (web-ifc) copied in via a postinstall step.

entries.seedReason: Private R&D POC tied to Structura. Enable only if comfortable publishing the approach.
entries.title: Structura — grout joint identification POC
entries.purpose: Proof of concept for auto-identifying and generating grout joint elements between adjacent BIM panels.
entries.methodologies: Deterministic geometry derivation between adjacent IFC elements; LLM used for orchestration rather than geometry.

entries.seedReason: Private venture (Servaa). Yours to expose, but confirm before publishing internals.
entries.title: Servaa
entries.purpose: AI/ML platform for the hospitality industry — reducing food waste, optimising staff rotas, and surfacing local produce sourcing insights for venue operators.

stack.api: tRPC v11 with TanStack Query integration (@trpc/tanstack-react-query) and SuperJSON
stack.data: Drizzle ORM over postgres-js against Supabase Postgres; Supabase SSR auth
stack.infra: Upstash Redis + @upstash/ratelimit for rate limiting; Vercel Analytics + Speed Insights
stack.testing: Vitest + Jest + Playwright (e2e); ESLint with jsx-a11y and react-hooks rules
stack.boundaries: server-only / client-only package guards to enforce the server/client split

entries.methodologies: Nx vertical-slice monorepo; strict type-safe API boundary (tRPC + Zod + SuperJSON); rate limiting at the edge with Upstash; layered testing (unit via Vitest/Jest, e2e via Playwright); accessibility linting.

entries.title: ThreadVault
entries.purpose: Construction QA evidence capture app built around preserving the 'Golden Thread' of documentation. Note: the current public repo is an Expo / React Native mobile app, which differs from the portfolio entry that describes a Next.js offline-first web tool.
entries.discrepancyNote: Portfolio PROJECTS tags ThreadVault as ['Next.js','TypeScript'] but tim-rayner/thread-vault is an Expo/React Native mobile project. Reconcile the portfolio tags or point the entry at the intended repo.

stack.framework: Expo (~56) + React Native 0.85 + Expo Router; React 19

entries.methodologies: File-based routing via expo-router; cross-platform (iOS/Android/web) from one codebase; worklet-driven animation.

entries.seedReason: Employer-owned (Artlist). Source is not in your personal repos and is proprietary. Seed only the public product description below, never internal code.
entries.title: Artlist AI Toolkit
entries.purpose: Creative-AI suite for content creators, video editors, and filmmakers — generate images, video, voiceovers, and music from text, or edit existing media. Used by millions of creatives. Tim worked on this as a frontend/fullstack engineer at Artlist.
entries.methodologies: Product engineering on a high-traffic creative-AI product; PRD/SDD-driven feature work; drag-and-drop and generation UX.

entries.title: React ESign
entries.purpose: Lightweight, dependency-free React component for capturing handwritten signatures — for e-signatures, form authentication, and user confirmations. Published to npm (v1.0.21), MIT licensed.

stack.build: Rollup producing CJS + ESM + type declarations (.d.ts)

entries.methodologies: Zero-runtime-dependency library design; dual-format (ESM/CJS) bundle with exported types; peer-dependency strategy; published and versioned on npm.

entries.seedReason: Private docs site for the public package. Low sensitivity; enable if you want the docs approach described publicly.
entries.title: React ESign — documentation site
entries.purpose: Documentation and live demo site for the react-esign package (hosted at react-esign-docs.vercel.app).
entries.methodologies: Docs + live demo separated from the library repo.

entries.seedReason: Private source for a shipped public Chrome extension. The extension itself is public on the Web Store; the source is private. Enable if you want implementation detail public.
entries.title: NoBizz
entries.purpose: Chrome extension that strips sponsored posts, engagement bait, and algorithmic filler from articles and blog posts, summarising why you clicked in seconds. Shipped on the Chrome Web Store.
entries.methodologies: Browser extension content-script architecture; client-side content extraction and summarisation.

entries.title: DreamDrive
entries.purpose: Creative-AI app for car enthusiasts: place your dream car in any Google Maps location and have AI generate a realistic, location-accurate render.

stack.ui: MUI v7 + Emotion, Framer Motion, Tailwind v4

entries.methodologies: Nx monorepo; serverless AI image generation via Replicate; Stripe-gated generation credits; S3-backed asset storage; map-driven prompt context.

entries.seedReason: Private. Uses Beryl GBFS data; confirm there is no third-party data/terms issue before publishing.
entries.title: Startline
entries.purpose: Beryl-powered sustainable commuting app — finds the best way to get to work based on location, weather, and commute preferences, using live bike-share (GBFS) data.

stack.data: Beryl GBFS feed wrapper (see companion beryl-tracker repo)

entries.methodologies: Wrapping a public GBFS micromobility feed; weather + location-aware routing.

entries.seedReason: Grouped non-sensitive summary of learning/practice repos. Safe to publish — demonstrates breadth and continuous learning.
entries.title: Learning & practice
entries.purpose: A body of public learning, certification, and practice repositories that demonstrate continuous skill development across languages and paradigms.
includes: Frontend Masters workshops — Intermediate React Native V2 and React Native V3 (Expo)
Vue 3 certification projects and advanced slots practice
A Spring/Java REST API portfolio project (Codecademy path)
Assorted micro-projects: custom OTP input, TS YouTube preview, WebSocket react-chat learning repo
entries.methodologies: Deliberate practice across React Native (Expo), Vue 3, Java/Spring, and algorithmic problem-solving; learning in public.
